stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 5 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 15 observations with mean 59.8 % (within-trial site SD = 6.8). trial site B has 20 observations with mean 40.6 % (within-trial site SD = 4.8). trial site C has 10 observations with mean 58.0 % (within-trial site SD = 3.4). trial site D has 5 observations with mean 61.3 % (within-trial site SD = 4.0). trial site E has 5 observations with mean 45.5 % (within-trial site SD = 9.7). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 5.0 and 12.6 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site E, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site E: 46.7 % (shrunk from raw 45.5). Range [46.1, 48.0] as between-trial site SD varies from 5.0 to 12.6. Pooled mean: 51.3.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 51.3 % (weighted average of 5 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site E: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=8.4: B = 0.789.
Interval [46.1, 48.0] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (45.5) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (51.3) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
